ActionItems: Hold studio operating spend under 600 per month.
Keep at least two retainer clients on the books each quarter.
Run one paid workshop cohort per quarter to cover fixed software costs.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Laura Creates Studio is a sole proprietorship. Income is project design work, monthly retainers, hand-lettering commissions, and quarterly workshops.

CurrentState.Note: Retainers cover fixed costs; project work is the variable upside. Home studio means no rent line.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Two monthly retainers plus one quarterly workshop cover all fixed costs, so every project fee is close to net.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: At 750 rent, fixed costs rise to 1270 per month; need two retainers plus four project fees to break even.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Renting a small studio space would erase most of the current reserve buffer.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Lease a part-time studio at 750 per month

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: An 850 studio lease requires a third retainer client to stay net positive.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Lease a larger studio at 850 per month
